title: Quiz: Quantum Methods in Drug Design
description: Test your understanding of quantum chemistry applications in drug discovery

questions.question: When is quantum chemistry essential rather than optional in drug design?

options.text: When modeling metalloproteins or covalent inhibitors
When classical MD is too slow

questions.explanation: Quantum chemistry is essential when force fields fundamentally fail: metalloprotein coordination, covalent bond formation, charge transfer, and electronic effects. Classical methods can handle most non-covalent protein-ligand interactions.

questions.question: What does SAPT (Symmetry-Adapted Perturbation Theory) provide?

options.text: A faster way to compute molecular energies
A decomposition of interaction energy into physical components

questions.explanation: SAPT provides a rigorous decomposition of non-covalent interaction energies into electrostatic, exchange, induction, and dispersion components. This gives mechanistic insight into what drives binding.

questions.question: Why do standard DFT methods fail for drug-protein π-π stacking interactions?

options.text: They use too many basis functions

questions.explanation: Standard DFT functionals do not capture London dispersion forces, which arise from electron correlation. This is why DFT-D3, DFT-D4, or functionals like ωB97X-D that include dispersion corrections are needed for π-stacking.

questions.question: In covalent inhibitor design, what does QM provide that force fields cannot?

options.text: Reaction barriers and covalent bond energies

questions.explanation: Force fields cannot model chemical reactions or bond formation. QM is required to calculate reaction barriers (ΔG‡), which determine kinact, and covalent bond energies that affect inhibitor reversibility and selectivity.

questions.question: Current quantum computers can routinely outperform classical methods for drug binding affinity calculations.
questions.explanation: False. Current NISQ (Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum) computers cannot yet outperform classical methods for practical drug discovery problems. Quantum advantage for drug discovery is expected in the late 2020s or beyond.

questions.question: Which are valid uses of QM in drug design? (Select all)

options.text: High-throughput screening of millions of compounds

questions.explanation: QM is valuable for pKa prediction, tautomer equilibria, and metalloprotein modeling. However, QM is too slow for high-throughput screening of millions of compounds - that requires fast empirical methods.
